intro: Dubyaea chimiliensis, also known as Chimili, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is used in traditional medicine, primarily for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Dubyaea chimiliensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dubyaea chimiliensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and ointments to treat various health conditions.
